deduplication_summary._note: Input contains 81 unique hypothesis IDs (8 TECH2 IDs appear twice due to data collision). Removal count is 60 unique IDs. TECH2-H007 retained despite one of its two instances being a duplicate of OPS-H015, because the other instance (Relayer Griefing via Inner Call Revert) is unique.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: transferFrom fee deduction bypasses ERC20 allowance — _payTxFee deducts fees from sender's balance via _update() before super.transferFrom checks/consumes allowance, allowing spender to extract amount + fee while only needing amount in allowance
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: _payTxFee calls _update(from, _feesFaucet, txFees) outside allowance accounting in Token.transferFrom, violating ERC20 allowance invariant. TECH-H001 selected for precise proof_of_signal, CRITICAL severity, and comprehensive technical analysis. Note: TECH2-H008 appears twice in input (second instance also targets this vulnerability).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Forwarder accepts arbitrary caller-supplied domainSeparator enabling cross-contract and cross-chain EIP-712 signature replay
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: Forwarder._verifySig uses domainSeparator parameter directly without validation against contract's own domain. TECH-H002 selected for clearest technical description of the EIP-712 domain binding bypass. Note: TECH2-H006 appears twice in input (second instance also targets this vulnerability).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Revoked controller can re-enable safety switch — safetySwitch re-enable path checks _operatingController == _msgSender() without verifying caller still holds CONTROLLER role
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: safetySwitch() re-enable branch at ERC20ControlerMinterUpgradeable.sol lacks hasRole(CONTROLLER) check, allowing revoked controllers to re-enable operations. TECH-H003 selected for precise proof_of_signal with exact line reference and clear preconditions. Note: TECH2-H003 appears twice in input (first instance is this vulnerability; second instance is a different vulnerability in Group DUP-015).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Mint and burn operations bypass pause and blacklist checks — mint() and burn() only check _operating flag and roles, not adminSanity/paused()/isBlacklisted()
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: mint() and burn() in ERC20ControlerMinterUpgradeable.sol do not call adminSanity(), paused(), or isBlacklisted(). ECON-H005 selected for comprehensive coverage of both mint and burn, both pause and blacklist bypass, NatSpec discrepancy, and economic impact analysis. Note: TECH2-H004 appears twice in input (first instance is this vulnerability).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MASTER_MINTER unexpectedly constrained by MINTER_ROLE allowance when holding both roles — mint() checks hasRole(MINTER_ROLE) independently without excluding MASTER_MINTER
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: mint() at ERC20ControlerMinterUpgradeable.sol applies allowance check for any MINTER_ROLE holder without excluding MASTER_MINTER. TECH-H004 selected for clearest code-level fix suggestion and precise proof_of_signal.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Phantom fee requirement blocks transfers when _feesFaucet is address(0) but _txfeeRate > 0 — balance check is unconditional but fee deduction is conditional on feesFaucet being set
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: _payTxFee checks balanceOf(from) < txFees + txAmount unconditionally but only executes _update when _feesFaucet != address(0). TECH-H005 selected for precise proof_of_signal, clear deployment context evidence, and complete precondition documentation. Note: TECH2-H005 and TECH2-H011 are from separate TECH2 runs but describe the same bug.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Shared nonce pool between permit() and transferWithAuthorization() enables cross-function signature invalidation via _useNonce sharing
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: both permit() and transferWithAuthorization() call _useNonce(owner) on the shared _nonces mapping. ECON-H002 selected for HIGH severity (vs MEDIUM), detailed economic impact analysis, and clear explanation of EIP-2612/EIP-3009 nonce domain separation violation. Note: TECH2-H010 appears twice in input (first instance is this vulnerability).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Forwarder verify() and execute() have inconsistent validation — verify() omits req.to == _eurfAddress check that execute() enforces, misleading relayers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: verify() checks nonce/sig/selector but NOT req.to == _eurfAddress, while execute() does. TECH-H007 selected for concise, precise proof_of_signal with exact line comparison. Note: TECH2-H010 appears twice in input (second instance is this vulnerability; first is DUP-007).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Admin role bypasses pause and sender blacklist in adminSanity() — compromised or blacklisted admin can still transfer tokens during emergency
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: adminSanity() wraps pause and sender blacklist checks inside if (!hasRole(ADMIN, _msgSender())), exempting admins from both. ECON-H018 selected for HIGH severity, clear economic attack analysis, and specific incident response failure scenario. Note: TECH2-H006 first instance and TECH2-H009 second instance target this vulnerability.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: setOwner brittle EnumerableSet logic — revokes OWNER from getRoleMember(OWNER, 0) which may not be the correct address when multiple owners exist
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: setOwner uses getRoleMember(OWNER, 0) for revocation, which is unreliable when EnumerableSet has >1 member. ECON-H006 selected for HIGH severity, comprehensive hidden-owner scenario, and UUPS upgrade risk analysis. Note: TECH2-H009 first instance targets this vulnerability; TECH2-H009 is listed in removal covering both instances.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: payGaslessBasefee bypasses pause and blacklist compliance checks — direct _update call without adminSanity when called by trusted forwarder
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: payGaslessBasefee() only checks isTrustedForwarder(msg.sender) then calls _update without adminSanity/pause/blacklist checks. OPS-H015 selected for HIGH severity and detailed operational analysis. Note: TECH2-H007 second instance and TECH2-H008 first instance target this vulnerability. TECH2-H007 is NOT listed in threats_to_remove because its first instance (Relayer Griefing via Inner Call Revert) is a unique, distinct vulnerability.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Public __ERC20ControlerMinter_init allows state reset during initialization contexts — declared public instead of internal, could reset token metadata and role grants during upgrades
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same root cause: __ERC20ControlerMinter_init is declared public onlyInitializing instead of internal onlyInitializing, deviating from OZ pattern. OPS-H008 selected for clearer description of specific state reset consequences (MASTER_MINTER granted to address(0), ERC20 reinitialization).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Role management functions (addController/removeController/addMinter/removeMinter/setMasterMinter) lack explicit access control modifiers — rely on internal grantRole/revokeRole checks which could be undermined by misconfiguration
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: external role-management functions call grantRole/revokeRole without explicit onlyRole modifiers, creating risk of privilege escalation if role admin config changes. TECH-H013 selected for comprehensive coverage of all five affected functions and detailed role-admin graph analysis. Note: TECH2-H004 second instance and TECH2-H005 second instance target subsets of this same issue.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee avoidance via dust transfer splitting — integer division in calculateTxFee truncates fee to zero for small amounts, enabling systematic fee evasion
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same root cause: (txAmount * _txfeeRate) / FEE_RATIO truncates to zero for small amounts, allowing fee-free transfers by splitting. ECON-H017 selected for clear proof_of_signal and economic threshold analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: setOwner() lacks explicit onlyRole modifier — relies on internal grantRole/revokeRole auth which could be bypassed under misconfiguration or role admin changes
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same root cause: setOwner(address) is public without onlyRole(OWNER) modifier, with authorization depending entirely on AccessControl role-admin state. OPS-H014 selected for unique hypothesis ID (TECH2-H003 collides with the revoked controller vulnerability in DUP-003). Note: TECH2-H003 second instance targets this vulnerability; removing TECH2-H003 covers both instances.
